---
title: "CTA Tree Diagrams"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

**Purpose:** Show how to visualise CTA trees using the current native base-R
renderer. Explain `cta_plot_data()` as the renderer-independent data contract
and `plot.cta_tree()` as the current renderer. Demonstrate structural mode and
target-enriched mode. Note what endpoint colours encode (relative target-class
proportion within the tree only) and what they do not imply. Describe planned
future ggplot/grid renderer.

**Source material:**
- `docs/ODACORE_VISION.md` — graphics section
- `docs/CTA_TRANSLATION_STACK.md` — `cta_plot_data()` / `plot.cta_tree()` entries

**Eval policy:** CTA_DEMO structural plot is live (small dataset, mc_iter = 500).
Myeloma target-enriched plot is live on precomputed fit loaded from
`inst/extdata/articles/myeloma_fits.rds`. Both plots render inline via base-R;
no separate figure artifacts needed.

**Precomputed artifacts needed:** `inst/extdata/articles/myeloma_fits.rds`
(not yet generated — Slice 3E).

---

*This article is not yet expanded. Placeholder sections are listed below.*

## `cta_plot_data()`: the renderer-independent data contract

## `plot.cta_tree()`: native base-R renderer

## Structural mode (no `target_class`)

## Target-enriched mode (`target_class`)

### What endpoint colours encode

### What endpoint colours do not imply

## Endpoint label content

## Current limitations and future renderer (v3)
